title: 5.3.2 Dijkstra Algorithm
subtitle: Single-source shortest path for non-negative weights

content.introduction: Dijkstra computes shortest paths from one source in graphs with non-negative edge weights using greedy distance expansion.

sections.title: 5.3.2.1 Key Ideas and Model
sections.content: Define settled vs unsettled nodes, tentative distance estimates, and the greedy selection invariant.
examples: Explain why non-negative weights are required
Interpret predecessor tree for path reconstruction

sections.title: 5.3.2.2 Representation and Workflow
sections.content: Describe priority-queue-based workflow: initialize source, repeatedly extract min-distance node, and relax outgoing edges.
examples: Run Dijkstra on a 5-node weighted graph
Show priority queue updates after each relaxation
Recover shortest route from predecessor links

sections.title: 5.3.2.3 Complexity and Practical Guidance
sections.content: Complexity is O((V+E) log V) with binary heap (or O(V^2) with array). Discuss correctness conditions and non-negative edge requirement.
examples: Compare heap vs array implementation complexity
Contrast Dijkstra with Bellman-Ford on negative edges
Use Dijkstra in map routing and network latency optimization

keyTakeaways: Analyze complexity under different data structures
Apply Dijkstra to practical routing tasks
